mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present value of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and weakening consumer demand, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or out-of-sample directional accuracy falls below 55% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer spending strength, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative forward returns in > 50% of occurrences, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression over 5-year out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase indicates capacity utilization approaching maximum limits, prompting equity markets to price in imminent supply bottlenecks and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Signal direction flips to positive during late-cycle phases, or predictive power vanishes (Rank IC < 0.02) when regime is correctly identified using a state-space filter.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings exceeding 3.0 percent trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns within the next trading month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of threshold-crossing months, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a logistic regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, supporting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between payroll growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or directional accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with stable consumer price indices creates a favorable macro environment that boosts equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction effect reverses sign or becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing, or forward returns are negative in > 55% of joint-signal occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production coinciding with increasing federal funds rates signals an overheating economy where monetary policy offsets supply-side gains, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative returns during tightening cycles (accuracy < 50%), or the joint effect is subsumed by single-series effects in a multivariate model (interaction p > 0.05).
